rules_evaluated.evidence: Bootstrap absolute ban is honored. Line 43 uses `practice` in context `'this is your practice now'` per AE101 carve-out language, but Bootstrap context does not carry the earned M4-equivalent. Review: line 43 'The transferable skill' and 44 'not freeform chat' are moves, not the noun practice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: No session-date markers, no 'MOVED' blocks, no 'fixed' annotations in body. Maintainer block carries design history per rule exception.

Bootstrap voice trio (Godin × Sutherland × Siilasmaa) consistent throughout. Line 4 reframe (Sutherland), line 8 absorbs-faster-than-access (Sutherland's counterintuitive), line 23 generous tone (Godin). Peer register maintained.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 43 introduces 'Agent reads your context, drafts a JTBD hypothesis' without crediting Clayton Christensen (Jobs-to-be-Done framework originator). Maintainer block at line 90 names 'thinking-disciplines' but does not surface the JTBD credit obligation into student-facing prose where the technique is first named. Rule 6 requires inline credit 'when natural'; this is the point of introduction.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add inline credit at line 43: 'Agent reads your context, drafts a JTBD hypothesis (Clayton Christensen's Jobs-to-be-Done pattern)' or relocate credit language from maintainer to body if integration feels natural in context.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Training creator's name banned in student-facing body.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: No `Antti`, `the creator`, `the author` (person-noun), `course creator`, `training creator` in body prose.

Lines using 'we': line 21 'our practice' (practices and contexts, not author-we); line 37 'our practice' (practitioner collective, not training-author collective); line 47 'our practice' (same). All three are ecosystem voice or framed example-from-practice, not author-we ('the training authors' substitution does not yield weirdness).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'the student' in body. Delivery mechanics (trainer cues, timing, demo setup) correctly placed below `<!-- maintainer -->`.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 49 'The pattern catalog' — pattern is earned inline ('Not a menu. You pick one to three patterns'). No maintainer-only jargon detected in body.
No reassurance-shaped sentences detected. Lines 33 ('four sharing shapes that work in practice') and 51 ('The real question isn't') are direct, not hedge-wrapped.

Line 37 contains em-dash: 'access far ahead of trust. People can reach the agent; that does not mean they know when to use it, trust it with real work, or have a way to encounter it in the flow of the day. You'll feel that gap yourself when your technical plan fills quickly and your people plan stalls on names.' — The sentence structure is correct but line 37 reads 'access is not absorption. In our practice, many companies have rolled out access far ahead of trust.' The em-dash should be replaced with period or comma per rule 14 (no em-dashes, simple sentences).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Line 37: Replace em-dash with period (two sentences) or comma + conjunctive. Example: 'access is not absorption. In our practice...' or 'Access is not absorption; in our practice...'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 39: `[Exercise: Share your work](exercises/share-your-work.md)` uses H1-title phrasing. Link is body-prose-style, not code-span path.
Module 7 prework establishes the generous mood ('The question that shows up on its own: could this help someone else?' line 23). Mood is deliberate, not flattened. Sets up Module 8 extension work.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: **Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode.**
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 4 Big Idea leads with discipline: 'You can't really share an agent. You can share context, a skill, the output, or an interface. The hard part is not access; it is absorption.' Frames discipline (designing for absorption) not failure (failed rollouts).
Fenced block at lines 59-65 is in maintainer region (after line 80 cut). Not evaluated as body prose.

summary: Two blocking issues: (1) Missing JTBD framework credit in body at line 43 (check_writing.md rule 6). (2) Em-dash in line 37 violates simple-sentence rule (check_student_facing.md rule 12). All other compendium rules PASS. File is otherwise register-aligned, voice-consistent, and structured correctly for Bootstrap mood arc.
